title: ismene pedunculata
intro: Ismene pedunculata is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, commonly known as the "Hawaiian Crinum" or "Island Crinum." The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The roots and flowers of Ismene pedunculata have been used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Consuming these herbal preparations is said to provide health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function, although further research is needed to confirm these effects.
